title: Dot-Dash Your Name Day
story: Every January 11, people celebrate Learn Your Name in Morse Code Day by turning their names into dots and dashes! Morse code uses short beeps (dots) and long beeps (dashes) to spell out letters. Kids love tapping out their names on tables, flashing phone lights in patterns, or even blinking the code with their eyes. Some families have fun contests to see who can morse their name the fastest. Teachers often use this day to show students how people sent secret messages before texting existed. It's amazing how three simple sounds can spell anything!
activity_question: What two symbols does Morse code use to represent letters?
follow_up_question: What would your name sound like if you tapped it out in Morse code?

title: Splash Happy: Puddle Jumping Day!
story: Every January 11, kids and grown-ups celebrate National Step in a Puddle and Splash Your Friends Day by doing exactly what it sounds like! People hunt for the biggest, muddiest puddles they can find and jump right in. The goal is to create the most epic splash possible and get your friends soaking wet. Some families organize puddle-jumping contests in their backyards or local parks. Others wear their oldest clothes and rain boots for maximum splashing power. Social media fills with videos of people leaping into puddles with huge grins on their faces. It's the perfect excuse to act like a little kid again and embrace getting completely messy!
activity_question: What do people wear for maximum splashing power on this holiday?
follow_up_question: What's the biggest splash you've ever made in a puddle?
